description: Test fixtures for pydepgate .pth parser. All synthetic_* fixtures use the structural shape of real attacks (matching MITRE T1546.018 techniques) but contain only inert payloads — print statements, subprocess calls that echo, URL requests to RFC 2606 reserved domains, etc. No actual malicious code is present.

benign_simple_path.pth.description: Single path entry, no trailing newline. Common real-world shape.

benign_legit_editable.pth.description: Shape of a real setuptools editable-install .pth. LEGITIMATE exec. Rules must allow this pattern (via allowlist) while flagging malicious patterns. Non-existent module name — parser must not care since it doesn't execute.

synthetic_base64_exec.pth.description: base64 + exec pattern (LiteLLM attack shape, inert payload).

synthetic_network.pth.description: Network pattern targeting RFC 2606 reserved domain (non-resolvable).

synthetic_oversized.pth.description: Oversized file (padded with comments). Generated programmatically — update this field if you regenerate with a different size.
synthetic_oversized.pth.notes: Uses a minimum-threshold check because exact size depends on generation parameters.

synthetic_multi_exec.pth.description: Multiple import lines — legitimate .pth files rarely have more than one.

synthetic_obfuscated_import.pth.description: String-split obfuscation to defeat naive pattern matching.

malformed_encoding.pth.description: Invalid UTF-8 bytes at file start. Parser must fall back to latin-1.

malformed_bare_cr.pth.description: Old-Mac-style bare \r line separators. Tests splitlines() correctness.

empty.pth.description: Zero-byte file. Parser must return empty ParsedPth without raising.

only_whitespace.pth.description: Lines containing only whitespace. All must classify as BLANK.
